keyPoints: Autonomy-supportive prompts prioritizing deliberate practice over blind AI reliance.
Confidently label outputs with core DNL components: confidence, estimated effort, and next steps.
Safeguards and opt-out options to prevent cognitive overload and preserve user control.
RCT-driven evaluation framing with experimental and factorial arms to parse granularity and advisor presets.
Primary outcomes: self-regulation, satisfaction, persistence, and AI-dependence proxies; secondary outcomes include performance and calibration.
Account for domain and user-context moderators (task difficulty, expertise) to map generalizability.
Ethical transparency, data provenance, and governance embedded in design and reporting.
